content.content:
Usage: nexus [options] [command]

Nexus AI CLI - WordPress site management with AI superpowers

Options:
  -V, --version           output the version number
  -h, --help              display help for command

Commands:
  sites                   Manage Local and WPE sites
  wp                      Run WP-CLI commands on sites
  sync                    Sync sites between local and WPE
  wpe                     Manage WP Engine accounts and installs
  blueprints              Manage Local blueprints
  update [options]        Update the CLI to the latest version
  fleet                   Fleet intelligence and analytics
  content                 Content and context management
  ai                      AI and connector management
  audit                   Site and plugin audits
  gateway                 AI gateway usage and cost reporting
  mcp                     MCP server management and agent setup
  skills                  Manage Claude Code skills for the Nexus CLI
  doctor [options]        Check system health and show setup status
  troubleshoot [options]  Diagnose and recover from common Nexus AI issues
  help [command]          display help for command

content.content:
Usage: nexus content [options] [command]

Content and context management

Options:
  -h, --help                         display help for command

Commands:
  search [options] <target> <query>  Search content within a site
  search-all [options] <query>       Search content across all sites
  structure [options] <target>       Get site file structure
  index-status [options] <target>    Get indexing status for a site
  list-indexed [options]             List all indexed sites
  index <target>                     Index content for a site (creates
                                     searchable database of posts, pages, and
                                     products)
  reindex <target>                   [Deprecated] Use "nexus content index"
                                     instead
  help [command]                     display help for command

content.text:
Here's what I found across your local indexed sites:

---

**Most relevant: "Faker Incorporated"** — this site has the most substantive e-commerce content:

- **"How to Get More Out of Your WooCommerce Store"** (post, score 0.52) — actual editorial content about WooCommerce optimization: "WooCommerce is the most popular e-commerce platform on the planet — but out of the box, it's just the beginning. Here's how we help our clients squeeze every dollar out of their WooCommerce stores."
- **"Services"** (page, score 0.52) — digital strategy page mentioning e-commerce context
- **"Shop"** (page) — has an active WooCommerce shop

**Other local sites with WooCommerce installed** (Shop pages, but no unique content):
- **The Curated Shelf** — Shop page
- **nexus-test-site** — Shop page
- **nexustestsite1** — Shop page
- **faker-incorporated** (appears twice, likely two indexed versions) — Cart + Shop pages

The WPE-indexed sites (`wpe-*`) mostly have default WooCommerce pages (`[woocommerce_my_account]`, `[woocommerce_cart]` shortcodes) — no substantive content there.

**Bottom line:** "Faker Incorporated" is your only local site with real WooCommerce-related editorial content. The others have WooCommerce installed but only default scaffolding pages.
